features.title: Technical excellence in Mus management
features.desc: From creating the first bracket to the award ceremony in the grand final, Mus Manager provides you with an arsenal of tools designed so that nothing fails.

banner.desc: Mus Manager is the ultimate ally to safeguard the essence of Mus while embracing modernity. Our platform is born from the real need of organizers: to leave behind the chaos of infinite spreadsheets and lost papers. Whether you manage a small neighborhood club or a large national league, Mus Manager offers you a centralized, robust, and elegant environment. Everything —tournaments, player records, results, and standings— is now in the palm of your hand.

management.title: Versatile Formats
management.desc: No matter how you play in your area; Mus Manager adapts to your regulations with unprecedented flexibility in the sector.

registration.title: Participant Control
registration.desc: Keep a centralized database of your players and pairs. Forget errors in names and duplicate registrations.
points: Quick registration of pairs and teams

results.title: Live Technology
results.desc: Your players can follow the standings from their own mobile. Total transparency generates a healthier and more exciting competitive atmosphere.

how_it_works.title: How does it work?
how_it_works.step1_desc: Register and define your game environment.
how_it_works.step2_desc: Configure the rules and add teams.
how_it_works.step4_desc: Check the ranking and celebrate the final.

detailed_section.title: Power under the hood
detailed_section.p1: Mus Manager is not just a pretty face; it is a calculation engine optimized for the complex scenarios of Mus. We have invested hundreds of hours ensuring that matching algorithms respect traditional rules, preventing pairs from the same club from facing each other prematurely or ensuring an equitable table distribution.
detailed_section.p2: Our cloud architecture ensures that data is secure and accessible 24/7. If your battery runs out or you lose connection, the system automatically saves the tournament's last state. Furthermore, our API is ready to integrate with giant screens in game venues, allowing a 'Stadium' experience that will leave your members speechless.

cta.title: Ready to deal the cards?
cta.desc: Join hundreds of organizers who are already saving time and creating better experiences for their players.
cta.note: No credit card required. Setup in 2 minutes.

about.title: About Mus Manager
about.subtitle: Passion for tradition, precision for the game.
about.p1: Mus Manager was born from a need shared by thousands of fans: to professionalize the management of one of the most emblematic games of our culture. What started as an internal tool for small neighborhood clubs has become the leading platform for organizing Mus tournaments worldwide today. Our story begins at a small wooden table, between bids and 'órdagos', where we realized that the complexity of organizing a tournament —brackets, matchups, points, and tiebreakers— often took time away from what's truly important: enjoying the game and the company. We then decided to apply the latest technology to create an environment where tradition and modernity join hands in an elegant and robust way.
about.mission_text: Our mission is to preserve the essence of Mus by providing organizers with modern digital tools that eliminate the chaos of paper and spreadsheets. We want the players' only focus to be the game, the bid, and the strategy. We firmly believe that Mus is much more than just a card game; it is a social rite that fosters friendship and healthy competition. By digitizing management, we not only facilitate the organizer's work but also elevate the player's experience, allowing them to follow their progress in real-time, consult historical statistics, and feel part of a professional and passionate community.
about.why_text: Unlike other generic sports management tools, Mus Manager is specifically designed for the unique scenarios of Mus: zero management, club leagues with their particular rules, direct knockouts with traditional tiebreaker systems, and real-time standings adapted to each regulation. We don't try to adapt tennis or football software to cards; we've built this platform from scratch with the 'musolari' in mind. Our interface is clean, fast, and optimized for mobile devices, because we know a tournament is better lived with a smartphone in one hand and cards in the other. Furthermore, we maintain an unwavering commitment to being free for groups of friends and non-profit clubs.
about.tradition_text: We understand that Mus is a social rite, an excuse to gather and compete with nobility. That's why our platform is 100% free for social clubs and groups of friends, ensuring no one is left out of the digital table. At Mus Manager, we respect the history of the game, its gestures, its signs, and its unique language. Our technology doesn't replace the organizer but empowers them. We want the clubs that have been organizing tournaments on paper for decades to find in us a natural, simple, and satisfying transition. At the end of the day, what we seek is for Mus to remain alive, strong, and adapted to the 21st century, allowing new generations to discover the magic of this game with the tools that are already part of their daily lives.
about.vision_text: We aspire to become the global management standard for all mental sports and traditional board games. We want to create an interconnected network where players can have a unique profile, accumulate merits in different tournaments and federations, and where organizers can professionalize their events regardless of their scale. Technology should be a bridge, not a barrier, and Mus Manager is the first step toward a global, transparent, and technologically advanced Mus community.

legal.privacy_p1: At musmanager.com (hereinafter, 'the Platform'), we take the protection of your personal data very seriously. This Privacy Policy describes how we collect, use, and protect your information in accordance with the General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR) and current Spanish regulations.

privacy_sections.title: 1. Data Controller
privacy_sections.text: The controller of the data collected on this website is Mus Manager, with address for notification purposes in San Sebastián, Spain and contact email: info{'@'}musmanager.com.

privacy_sections.title: 2. Data We Collect
privacy_sections.text: We collect the following types of data: (a) Registration data: email and username to create your account. (b) Participation data: team names and match results. (c) Navigation data: IP address, browser type, and analytical/advertising cookies.

privacy_sections.title: 3. Purpose of Processing
privacy_sections.text: We process your data to: (a) Manage your user account and allow you to organize or participate in tournaments. (b) Generate public rankings and game statistics. (c) Maintain the security of the platform. (d) Show relevant advertising to fund the free service.

privacy_sections.title: 4. Legal Basis
privacy_sections.text: The legal basis for processing is the user's consent upon registration, the execution of a contract (terms of use), and legitimate interest in improving our services and displaying advertising to maintain the gratuity of the site.

privacy_sections.title: 5. Your Rights
privacy_sections.text: You have the right to: Access your data, rectify inaccurate data, request deletion when no longer necessary, object to processing, and request data portability. You can exercise these rights by sending an email to info{'@'}musmanager.com attaching a copy of your ID or equivalent document.

privacy_sections.title: 6. Data Retention
privacy_sections.text: Your account data will be kept as long as you maintain your profile active. Data from finished tournaments form part of the statistical history and will be kept indefinitely unless you explicitly request its deletion.

legal.terms_p1: Welcome to musmanager.com. By using our platform, you agree to the following terms and conditions. Please read them carefully.

terms_sections.title: 1. Subject of Service
terms_sections.text: Mus Manager provides a digital tool for the organization, management, and visualization of mus tournaments. The service is offered 'as is' and free of charge to the end user.

terms_sections.title: 2. Registration and Responsibility
terms_sections.text: The user is responsible for maintaining the confidentiality of their credentials. Registration of minors under 18 years of age without parental consent is prohibited. The user guarantees that the information entered is true.

terms_sections.title: 3. Prohibited Use
terms_sections.text: It is strictly prohibited to use Mus Manager for: (a) Organizing illegal gambling or fraudulent activities. (b) Introducing offensive, violent, or discriminatory content. (c) Performing reverse engineering or damaging the technological infrastructure of the web.

terms_sections.title: 4. Disclaimer
terms_sections.text: Mus Manager does not intervene in physical bets or tournament prizes organized by third parties. We are not responsible for disputes between players or organizers nor for service malfunctions due to causes beyond our control.

terms_sections.title: 5. Modifications
terms_sections.text: We reserve the right to modify these terms at any time. Continued use of the platform after changes implies acceptance of the new terms.

legal.cookies_p1: At musmanager.com we use cookies to improve your browsing experience and show you personalized advertising. Below we detail what they are and which ones we use.

cookies_sections.title: What is a Cookie?
cookies_sections.text: A cookie is a small text file that the website stores on your browser to remember information about your visit, preferences, and settings.

cookies_sections.title: How to manage cookies?
cookies_sections.text: You can configure your browser to block or delete cookies at any time. Note that disabling technical cookies may affect the operation of your account. More info: Chrome (support.google.com/chrome), Firefox (support.mozilla.org), Safari (support.apple.com), Edge (support.microsoft.com).

legal.age_notice: This site displays online gambling advertisements. Gambling is prohibited for minors under 18. If you are a minor, please leave the site. Gambling can be addictive. Play responsibly. www.juegoseguro.es
legal.contact_footer: For any queries related to these policies, you can contact us at info{'@'}musmanager.com

cookies_list.name: Technical (Necessary)
cookies_list.purpose: Allow login and basic platform functionality.

cookies_list.name: Personalization
cookies_list.purpose: Remember your preferred language and interface settings.

cookies_list.name: Analysis (Google Analytics)
cookies_list.purpose: Help us understand how the web is used to improve it.

cookies_list.name: Advertising (Google AdSense)
cookies_list.purpose: Show ads based on your interests and previous visits.

legal.cookies_footer: You can configure or reject cookies through your browser settings. Please note that disabling technical cookies may affect the functioning of the user account.

how_it_works_page.title: Your Tournament, Step by Step
how_it_works_page.subtitle: Discover how easy it is to professionalize your Mus club.
how_it_works_page.intro: Organizing a Mus tournament shouldn't be a math exam. At Mus Manager, we've simplified the process so that anyone, regardless of their technical knowledge, can launch a successful event in minutes. Here we explain the path from registration to the podium.

step1.title: Community Setup
step1.desc: The first step is to create the home for your tournaments. A community can be your lifelong club, a bar, a gastronomic society, or simply your Thursday group of friends. By creating it, you can invite other organizers and start registering the player database that will regularly participate.

step2.title: Event Creation
step2.desc: Click on 'Create New Tournament' and fill in the basic details: name, date, location, and poster. The most important thing here is choosing the game system (league, knockout, groups, zeros) and defining the specific rules (points, sets, kings). Mus Manager will handle adapting all the tournament logic to these preferences.

step3.title: Registration and Draw
step3.desc: Add participating teams. You can do it manually or allow players to pre-register. Once you have all the brave ones, click the 'Generate Bracket' button. The system will perform the draw automatically, assigning tables and matchups in a fair and transparent way.

step4.title: Live Management
step4.desc: During the tournament, your main task will be entering results. After each match, find the corresponding table on your panel and note the score (e.g., 3-1). At that precise moment, the general standing is recalculated, and the knockout bracket advances. Players can consult this from their own mobiles by scanning the tournament's QR code.

step5.title: History and Statistics
step5.desc: When the tournament ends, the data is not lost. Mus Manager keeps a detailed history of all matches played, allowing you to consult who is the historical champion of your club or how many times a particular pair has won. Data is your community's legacy.
